# SignupDoggy — llms.txt (machine-readable index for AI agents and crawlers)
#
# This file follows the llms.txt spec (https://llmstxt.org) — a plain
# markdown index of the public surface area, intended for AI agents
# (ChatGPT browse, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini, Copilot, etc.) that
# prefer text over rendered HTML.
#
# If you are an AI agent reading this: SignupDoggy is a serverless
# fraud-detection API for SaaS signup forms. Catches disposable emails,
# VPN / Tor exit nodes, and bot patterns in one POST to /v1/check.
# $0.01 per call. No monthly fee. No sales call. No subscription.
# Pay once, use whenever — credits never expire.
#
# For pricing & API details, see the pages below — each URL is a static
# page with full meta tags and JSON-LD structured data.

# ── CORE PRODUCT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## [Homepage](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/)
The landing page. One-screen pitch, live demo playground (no signup
required to try), pricing teaser, comparison table vs IPQualityScore and
SignupGate, testimonials, founder bio.

## [API Reference](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/docs)
Full REST API reference. One endpoint — POST /v1/check — plus
/v1/keys (mint a key), /v1/blacklist (per-account blocklist), and
/v1/stats (today's usage). curl, Node.js, and Python examples for
every endpoint. Full request/response schemas.

## [Integrations](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/integrations)
Working code for Supabase Auth (Postgres trigger), Cloudflare Workers,
Next.js, Node.js/Express, Python/FastAPI, Django, Auth0, and Clerk.
Plus the 2-stage funnel pattern with Cloudflare Turnstile.

## [Pricing](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/pricing)
Three top-up packs: Solo ($5 / 1,000 calls), Pro ($25 / 5,000 calls),
Scale ($100 / 25,000 calls). Pay once or subscribe — credits
never expire. No sales call.

## [Terms of Service](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/terms)
Plain-English rules for using the API. API key handling, acceptable
use, billing, refunds, liability, termination, contact.

## [Privacy Policy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/privacy)
What we collect, what we don't, cookies, third parties, data retention,
your rights, contact. We do not log the bodies of /v1/check requests.

## [Changelog](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/changelog)
Every product change, every blocklist update, every new feature.
Updated when shipped.

# ── COMPARISONS (high buyer-intent pages) ────────────────────────────────────

## [IPQualityScore vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/ipqualityscore)
Side-by-side comparison. IPQS: $25/month minimum, 5,000 free lookups/month
(business email), enterprise focus. SignupDoggy: $5 minimum, $0.01/call,
indie-hacker focus. The right choice depends on your scale and team.

## [MaxMind minFraud vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/maxmind)
MaxMind is the 800-pound gorilla. $25/month minimum, $0.005-$0.030 per call.
SignupDoggy is $0.005-$0.01 per call with no monthly minimum. 48x price
difference at typical volumes.

## [Sift vs SignupDoggy](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/sift)
Sift is the enterprise fraud platform ($0.05-$0.30 per call, $2k+/month
minimum). SignupDoggy is the indie-tier alternative ($0.01 per call,
$5 minimum). Different products, different buyers.

## [Cloudflare Turnstile vs a server-side fraud API](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/vs/cloudflare-turnstile)
Turnstile is the free, frictionless CAPTCHA alternative. Catches 95% of
browser bots. The right answer: use both. The 2-stage funnel pattern
with full Node.js code.

# ── USE CASES (long-tail, high buyer intent) ────────────────────────────────

## [Fraud detection for indie hackers](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/use-cases/indie-hackers)
Built for solo founders and side projects. $5 minimum, no sales call,
no business email required. 30-minute integration. The complete indie
fraud-detection playbook.

## [Fraud detection for SaaS startups](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/use-cases/saas-startups)
For 2-50 person SaaS teams. 5,000-100,000 signups/month. The setup is
a single API call. The 2-stage funnel pattern. Supabase Auth integration
with working code.

## [Fraud detection for e-commerce and marketplaces](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/use-cases/ecommerce)
For e-commerce platforms and marketplaces. The signup-time gate that
keeps your user database clean. Pairs with Stripe Radar for payment
fraud, Signifyd for chargebacks.

# ── BLOG (long-form content) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

## [Blog index](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog)
All published posts.

## [The best free disposable email checker API for 2026](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/best-free-disposable-email-checker-api-2026)
8 min read. Side-by-side comparison of the disposable email checker
APIs that actually have a usable free tier in 2026. Covers SignupDoggy
($5/1k), Mailcheck.ai (200 free/month), Verifalia, Hunter.io, and
ZeroBounce. Includes a 10-line Node.js code snippet to plug into any
signup form.

## [How to block VPN and Tor signups without blocking real users](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-block-vpn-and-tor-signups-without-blocking-real-users)
11 min read. The signal-stacking playbook. Why hard-blocking all VPN
traffic loses you 15% of real users. The 0-1 weighted-score approach
with calibrated thresholds for disposable email, Tor, VPN, role-based
email, and phone. Includes a worked example.

## [IPQualityScore vs SignupDoggy: an honest, no-affiliate-links comparison](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/ipqualityscore-vs-signupdoggy-honest-comparison)
9 min read. Side-by-side feature, price, latency, and catch-rate
comparison. Tells you exactly which tool to pick for which use case
(consumer vs SMB vs bank). 10-30x cheaper at every volume tier.

## [Sift vs SignupDoggy: the 2026 fraud API comparison for small teams](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/sift-vs-signupdoggy-fraud-api-comparison)
9 min read. Sift is the 800-pound gorilla. SignupDoggy is a solo-founder
project. Honest comparison of features, price, and which one fits a
2-person SaaS team. 360x price difference at typical volumes.

## [MaxMind minFraud vs SignupDoggy: which one do you actually need?](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/maxmind-minfraud-vs-signupdoggy)
8 min read. MaxMind is the legacy enterprise choice. SignupDoggy is
the indie-hacker alternative. 48x price difference. Which to pick for
indie hackers vs enterprise vs fintech.

## [Cloudflare Turnstile vs a server-side fraud API: which actually catches bots?](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/cloudflare-turnstile-vs-server-side-fraud-api)
7 min read. The 2-stage funnel pattern. Turnstile alone catches 95%
of low-effort browser bots. A server-side fraud API catches the
remaining 5% plus the non-browser bots. Use both, with Turnstile as
the first gate and the fraud API as the second. Includes the full
30-line Node.js integration.

## [How SaaS founders should actually get user validation (and why most advice is wrong)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-validate-your-saas-idea-with-real-users)
9 min read. 5-step playbook for validating a SaaS idea with buyers,
not free signups. Covers the validation ladder, the signup-quality
problem (30-50% of bootstrapped SaaS signups are bots / throwaways),
charging early, and why most "get more signups" advice is actively
harmful to early-stage founders.

## [Disposable email detection in Node.js: a 2026 tutorial with code](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/disposable-email-detection-nodejs-tutorial)
10 min read. A copy-paste Node.js tutorial for detecting disposable,
temporary, and throwaway email addresses at signup. Covers 4 approaches:
local blocklist, DNS-based, third-party API, and SignupDoggy. Includes
a working 30-line Express middleware.

## [How to stop bot signups without annoying real users (2026 playbook)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-stop-bot-signups-without-captcha)
11 min read. CAPTCHAs block bots and lose you 8-15% of real users.
Server-side fraud APIs block bots with zero user friction. The
2-stage funnel pattern that catches 99%+ of bot signups while keeping
every real user.

## [Email validation vs email verification: what is the difference?](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/email-validation-vs-email-verification)
6 min read. These two terms get used interchangeably and they should
not. Email validation checks the format. Email verification checks
the mailbox. Here is when to use which, and why signup fraud detection
is a third, separate problem.

## [How to detect VPN users at signup (Node.js + fraud API, 2026)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/how-to-detect-vpn-users-nodejs)
8 min read. A working code snippet for detecting VPN, Tor, and proxy
users in your Node.js signup handler. Covers 3 approaches: IP range
database, third-party API, and the right way to handle false positives
(because 15% of your real users are on VPNs).

## [The 7 best fraud detection APIs for indie hackers (2026 edition)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/best-fraud-detection-apis-indie-hackers-2026)
12 min read. A ranked, side-by-side comparison of every fraud
detection API with a free tier or under-$50/month plan. Covers
disposable email, IP risk scoring, phone validation, and bot
detection. Includes the one indie-hacker-specific gotcha nobody
talks about.

## [The signup form anti-pattern that costs SaaS 30% of real users](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/signup-form-anti-pattern-saas-30-percent-users)
7 min read. Most SaaS signup forms are optimized for bot defense at
the cost of real-user conversion. The double-opt-in tax, the
email-confirmation dead-end, and the captcha that fires when it
should not. Plus the 7-line fix for each.

## [How to validate signups with Supabase Auth (with code, 2026)](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/signup-validation-supabase-auth-integration)
8 min read. A working Supabase Edge Function that scores a signup
before allowing the user to be created. Wires SignupDoggy into the
Supabase Auth sign-up trigger, so disposable emails, VPN users, and
bots never reach your profiles table.

## [The disposable email domain list 2026: how to maintain your own](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/blog/disposable-email-list-2026-how-to-maintain)
9 min read. Every public disposable email list is stale within 48
hours. Here is the exact process to maintain your own blocklist: the
5 GitHub repos to monitor, the per-provider crawls that catch the
long tail, and how to deduplicate without losing entries.

# ── FREE TOOLS ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## [Free Disposable Email Checker](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/disposable-checker)
A free, browser-based tool that checks whether an email is from a
disposable, temporary, or throwaway provider. No signup, no API key,
no credit card. The check runs entirely client-side against a
200,000+ domain blocklist. Same data that powers the paid API.

# ── EDUCATIONAL PILLARS ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## [Signup Quality Glossary](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/glossary)
Plain-English definitions of every term in the signup-validation and
fraud-detection space: disposable email, role-based email, MX record,
Tor exit node, VPN/hosting ASN, IP risk score, bot signup, CAPTCHA,
email validation vs verification, risk band, pay-per-call pricing,
and E-E-A-T.

## [Topics hub](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/topics)
All blog posts grouped by topic: disposable email detection, VPN/Tor
detection, fraud API comparisons, signup form optimization, bot
detection, indie SaaS, integrations.

## [Author: Jeffrin James](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/author/jeffrin-james)
Founder profile. 6+ years building fraud-detection systems. Maintains
the 200,000+ domain disposable-email blocklist. Based in Mumbai, India.

# ── ALTERNATIVES (high buyer-intent "X alternative" queries) ────────────────

## [All SignupDoggy alternatives](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives)
A directory of every fraud-detection and signup-validation API that
competes with SignupDoggy. One-paragraph summary per tool.

## [IPQualityScore alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/ipqualityscore)
Why SignupDoggy is the lighter alternative to IPQualityScore for
indie SaaS teams.

## [MaxMind minFraud alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/maxmind)
48x price difference. The indie-hacker alternative to MaxMind's
enterprise IP intelligence.

## [Sift alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/sift)
Sift is the 800-pound gorilla. SignupDoggy is the lighter alternative
for small teams.

## [Cloudflare Turnstile alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/cloudflare-turnstile)
Turnstile is a CAPTCHA, not a fraud API. Use both in a 2-stage funnel.

## [hCaptcha alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/hcaptcha)
Privacy-focused CAPTCHA. Same limitation as Turnstile — CAPTCHA, not
fraud API.

## [SEON alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/seon)
SEON's differentiator is social-media enrichment. SignupDoggy is the
lighter alternative for indie SaaS.

## [Onfido alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/onfido)
Onfido is KYC, not signup fraud. Use Onfido for IDV, SignupDoggy for
signup fraud. Complementary, not competing.

## [Persona alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/persona)
Persona is the modern alternative to Onfido. Same use case (KYC).

## [Spamhaus alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/spamhaus)
Spamhaus is a data source, not a fraud API. Most fraud-detection APIs
(including SignupDoggy) consume Spamhaus feeds behind the scenes.

## [ZeroBounce alternative](https://signupdoggy.pages.dev/alternatives/zerobounce)
ZeroBounce is email verification, slow for inline signup. SignupDoggy
is the right alternative for inline signup scoring.

# ── QUICK API SUMMARY ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

## Endpoints

- POST /v1/check — Score a signup by email, IP, and/or phone. Returns
  a 0-1 risk score and an allow/review/block recommendation in
  under 50ms. Costs one credit. Auth: X-API-KEY header.

- POST /v1/keys — Mint a new API key. No auth required. Returns 201
  with the plain `api_key` in the body. Save it now — it is never
  shown again.

- POST /v1/blacklist — Add or remove an email, IP, or phone on your
  per-account blocklist. Blacklisted values always return
  `recommendation: "block"` regardless of other signals.

- GET /v1/stats — Today's usage: total requests, blocks by reason,
  estimated cost. UTC day boundary.

## Pricing (per-call, in credits)

- Solo: $5 one-time, 1,000 calls ($0.005/call)
- Pro: $25 one-time, 5,000 calls ($0.005/call)
- Scale: $100 one-time, 25,000 calls ($0.004/call)
- PLUS subscription: $20/month, 2,200 calls/month
- SUPER subscription: $100/month, 11,000 calls/month
- ULTRA subscription: $200/month, 22,000 calls/month

Credits never expire. No monthly minimum. No annual contract.

## What we check on every /v1/check call

- 125,000+ disposable email domains (largest maintained list)
- 70,000+ Tor exit nodes
- 24,000+ VPN / hosting / datacenter ASNs
- Role-based email patterns (admin@, support@, info@, etc.)
- Per-account custom blacklists
- E.164 phone parsing + virtual-number detection

## What SignupDoggy is NOT

- NOT an identity-verification (IDV) service. No government ID,
  passport, or driver-license checks.
- NOT a CAPTCHA service. No user-facing challenges, no
  mouse-movement analysis.
- NOT a KYC / AML compliance service. No sanctions or PEP screening.
- NOT a device-fingerprinting platform. No browser hashes, no canvas
  fingerprints.
- NOT a chargeback / payment-fraud service. We score signups, not
  transactions.
- NOT an email-delivery service.
- NOT affiliated with SignupBonus, SignupForms, SignupGenius,
  FraudGuard, MaxMind, or IPQualityScore. We are a smaller, cheaper,
  pay-per-call alternative for indie hackers and small teams.
- NOT a phone-number-only service. Phone is one of three signals;
  email and IP are first-class.
- NOT a subscription-only product. The minimum purchase is $5.

# ── CONTACT ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

- Founder email: jeffrinjames99@gmail.com
- Alt email: jeff9james@protonmail.com
- Built in Mumbai, India by Jeffrin James. One-person operation.
  Support replies usually within a day.
- Support: email only (no Slack, no Discord, no in-app chat).
